articles.title: YOUTUBE IS GIVING SOME CHANNELS A NETFLIX MAKEOVER
articles.summary: YouTube is experimenting with a Netflix-style interface makeover for select channels through its new Shows feature. The redesign features supersized thumbnails, episode descriptions, "Watch Now" buttons, and scheduled content shelves that resemble streaming services. The UX aims to help creators with connected content appear more like the programming traditional TV networks produce.

articles.title: APPLE WATCH ULTRA 3'S LARGER SCREEN SIZE LEAKS IN NEW IOS 26 BETA
articles.summary: The upcoming Apple Watch Ultra 3 is expected to feature a slightly larger display with a new 422 x 514 resolution, as revealed in the iOS 26 beta, suggesting either a bigger case or slimmer bezels. Rumored upgrades include satellite messaging, high blood pressure detection, a new LTPO3 OLED display, 5G support, and a next-gen S10 or S11 chip. It will likely be unveiled at Apple's September 9 event.

articles.title: APPLE'S LATEST LIQUID GLASS DESIGN SHOWS IT WON'T BACK DOWN WITH IOS 26
articles.summary: iOS 26's standout feature is the new Liquid Glass design. Despite some early backlash during the beta cycle, Apple has doubled down on its original vision, refining and expanding it with each release. With the latest beta showing minimal changes and Apple's typical pattern of locking in final designs by August, Liquid Glass is all but confirmed to ship in next month's official release, with potential refinements likely to follow in later updates.

articles.title: WHAT DESIGNING AI-CENTRIC PRODUCTS LOOKS LIKE NOW AND IN THE (NEAR) FUTURE
articles.summary: The conversational AI market faces explosive growth from £9.2B to £46.4B by 2032, despite only 46% of users trusting AI systems. Traditional interfaces are shifting toward natural language commands, fundamentally reimagining how humans interact with digital products and systems. Future AI-centric products will likely emphasize strong personalities and gamification while requiring designers to balance enhancement with unnecessary complexity.

articles.title: WHAT I LOOK FOR IN DESIGN PORTFOLIOS (AND WHAT MOST PEOPLE MISS)
articles.summary: Design portfolios stand out in today's competitive job market through strong craft, exceptionally high-quality typography, and creative assets that build trust before interviews begin. The medium itself becomes the message, with thoughtful presentation proving standards and credibility to hiring managers. Portfolios should proactively answer common interview questions about leadership style and approach rather than waiting to be asked.

articles.title: HAVE DESIGN JOB ADS BECOME TOO SPECIFIC?
articles.summary: Designers are increasingly frustrated by overly specific job listings that demand sector-specific experience, agency backgrounds, or even niche project types—requirements seen as rigid, exclusionary, and reflective of a risk-averse industry. While some recruiters argue this isn't new, the economic downturn, competitive job market, and social media visibility have amplified the issue, prompting growing debate between hiring for safety versus valuing fresh perspectives.

articles.title: INSTANTLY TURN ANY DOCUMENTS AND IDEAS INTO ANY DIAGRAM
articles.summary: Fluig AI empowers you to transfer any docs or creative ideas into clear, professional diagrams in seconds, easily converting between diagram formats in one click.

articles.title: BUILD MOBILE APPS FROM TEXT PROMPTS
articles.summary: DreamFlow is a conversational AI that builds complete mobile apps from text prompts. Get fully-functional Flutter apps with AI integrations, database integration, and multi-platform deployment.

articles.title: FIGMA UI KIT AND DESIGN SYSTEM
articles.summary: Create beautiful, high-performance websites faster than ever before with seamless workflows for Webflow, Framer, and Figma.

articles.title: FIGMA CEO'S PATH FROM COLLEGE DROPOUT AND THIEL FELLOW TO TECH BILLIONAIRE
articles.summary: Dylan Field dropped out of Brown University over a decade ago to join the Thiel Fellowship program and co-founded design software company Figma. Figma's stock more than tripled in its NYSE debut this week, making Field's stake worth approximately $6.6 billion after a failed $20 billion Adobe acquisition. The company now focuses on AI-powered features like Figma Make, which uses Anthropic's Claude model to generate coded prototypes from design prompts.

articles.title: A TOP DESIGNER WAS BANNED FROM DRIBBBLE. NOW HE'S BUILDING HIS OWN COMPETITOR
articles.summary: Dribbble permanently banned dozens of designers, including prominent designer Gleb Kuznetsov, following new marketplace rules that require revenue sharing. Kuznetsov's 210 million-follower account was deleted after he shared contact information with clients, violating the platform's updated terms. The San Francisco-based studio founder now plans to launch an AI-focused competitor platform within three to four months.

articles.title: 'THINKING DIFFERENTLY IS A SUPERPOWER': SIMON DIXON ON HOW DIXONBAXI WAS BUILT
articles.summary: DixonBaxi built long-term success through patient persistence, clear values, and bold decisions like resigning misaligned clients. Rather than chasing trends or profit, it thrives by designing with purpose, fostering a human-centered, collaborative culture, and actively shaping its future through initiatives like creative sabbaticals and inclusive innovation.

articles.title: CAN YOU GET MORE THAN 1 OUT OF 16 IN THIS IMPOSSIBLE LOGO DESIGN QUIZ?
articles.summary: A Reddit user created an extremely challenging quiz featuring 16 swoosh logos that look frustratingly similar and generic.

articles.title: GPD WIN 5 BRINGS A BOLD REDESIGN THAT CHALLENGES HANDHELD GAMING NORMS
articles.summary: GPD's upcoming WIN 5 handheld represents a dramatic redesign that abandons the series' signature slider keyboard for a streamlined, controller-focused gaming device.
